SYSTEM ANALYSIS:

Status: The toolchain stages complete and link on Darwin (_main emitted). Expression routing and byte loads are correct; the program now crashes at runtime.

Root cause: Code generation computes the address for argv[1] without scaling the index by the element size. The emitted sequence shows `mov x9, #1; add x10, x1, x9; ldr x9, [x10]`, which adds 1 byte to argv (char**), yielding a bogus pointer and leading to `ldrb w9, [x11]` EXC_BAD_ACCESS. This is an architectural gap in array-index address calculation within codegen (generate_assembly pipeline): element size must come from the base’s type annotation (pointee). On AArch64, pointer elements are 8 bytes (shift 3). For integers, use bit_width/8. Apply `add addr, base, idx, LSL #shift` when size is a power of two; leave unscaled for size 1. Keep existing load width logic (ldrb/ldrh/ldr wN/ldr xN).

Next steps: Update the array-index address computation in the code generation path under generate_assembly/traverse_ast_post to derive element size from type annotations and emit scaled adds (LSL #3 for pointers). Re-run to verify the crash is resolved and outputs match expected results.
